title: Tracking: ferray + ferrolearn integration roadmap (umbrella for #564-#571)
description:
Umbrella issue tracking the phased ferray + ferrolearn integration work. Source audit: docs/audit/16-ferray-ferrolearn-integration.md.

Phase 1 (immediate, ferray subcrates already published):
- #564 Phase 1A: ferray-fft -> ferrotorch-core::fft (10 missing fft fns)
- #565 Phase 1B: ferray-linalg eig/lstsq/lu/etc. -> ferrotorch-core::linalg + cuSOLVER GPU dispatch (~25 fns) — biggest single API gap
- #566 Phase 1C: ferray-window -> ferrotorch-core::signal::windows (torch.signal.windows top-level gap)
- #567 Phase 1D: ferray-polynomial -> ferrotorch-core::special polynomial families
- #568 Phase 1E: ferray-stride-tricks -> Tensor::as_strided + as_strided_copy/scatter

Phase 2 (medium, broader features):
- #569 Phase 2A: ferray-ma -> ferrotorch-core::masked (torch.masked top-level gap)
- #570 Phase 2B: feature-gated ferray-numpy-interop -> ferrotorch-data Arrow/Polars interop

Phase 3 (new crate, sklearn bridge):
- #571 Phase 3: create ferrotorch-ml workspace crate (Tensor <-> ndarray adapter for ferrolearn metrics/preprocess/CV/decomposition)

Cross-cutting GPU discipline (applies to every phase):
- Each issue includes explicit /rust-gpu-discipline pre-flight: forbidden patterns, mechanical verification, honest reporting.
- Forbidden across the board: silent t.to(Cpu) inside compute functions, then back to device. ferray and ferrolearn are CPU-only — that's fine, but the boundary must be visible to the caller.
- Phase 1B (linalg) is the only phase that REQUIRES new GPU code (cuSOLVER dispatch via existing ferrotorch-gpu::cusolver). The other phases are CPU-with-explicit-error-on-GPU.

Phases can land independently. Recommended order: 1A and 1E first (small, no GPU work), then 1B (biggest impact, needs GPU plumbing), then 1C/1D/2A/2B in parallel, then Phase 3 once the core surface stabilizes.

Reference docs/audit/README.md and docs/audit/16-ferray-ferrolearn-integration.md for context.
